regimeNotes: NEUTRAL regime maintained 2026-07-15, with elevated caution flags. Geopolitics/oil: the US-Iran conflict re-escalated over the weekend of July 12-13 (US struck 80+ targets inside Iran) and Trump reinstated the naval blockade of Iranian ports/Strait of Hormuz on July 14, with only ~6 vessels crossing the strait vs. 18-22/day normally and ~230 loaded tankers stranded in the Gulf -- a genuine supply-shock catalyst. Oil: WTI ~$79-80, Brent ~$84-85 (Brent's biggest one-day gain in 6+ years on July 13). Rates: 10Y yield jumped to ~4.62% (2-month high) on the oil/inflation scare; futures now price a 25bp hike as soon as October with a second in April 2027 (up sharply from just one hike by December priced a week earlier) -- new Fed Chair Warsh emphasizing price stability, a hawkish tightening signal. Core CPI running ~2.8% y/y, still above target, though headline CPI cooled to 3.5% in the July 13 print (initially DXY-negative before the Hormuz escalation reversed sentiment). DXY ~100.8, roughly flat/soft. Equities: resilient despite the above -- SPY $751.83 (S&P 500 ~7,540s, near record), VIX calm at ~16.5 (well under the 20 stress line), semis/AI names (NVDA +4% to $211.80, AVGO $389, META $661) leading -- market has not yet priced meaningful risk from the war/rate developments. Crypto: BTC $64,777 (roughly flat vs. July 8's $63,325), ETH $1,883.72 (+5.8%), SOL $77.60 (-4.7%), total crypto mcap ~$2.30T (+2.6% 24h) yet Fear & Greed fell to 25 'Extreme Fear' (from 27 'Fear') -- sentiment deteriorating even as prices firm, a notable divergence. BTC dominance rose to 56.3% (from 55.8%). NAV $100,905.64 (+1.07% vs July 8), all-time high for the book. Crypto sleeve drifted to 20.18% of NAV on BTC/ETH strength, marginally above the 20% neutral cap -- trimmed 2.6 SOL (~$202) to bring it to 19.98%. Cash 13.58% of NAV, comfortably above the 5% floor. No position exceeds the 15% entry cap (MSFT largest at 14.53%) and none breach the ~20% drift-trim trigger. No invalidation levels breached. Net read: a real geopolitical/inflation risk-off catalyst (Hormuz blockade, hawkish hike repricing) is building underneath a still-resilient, near-record equity tape and low VIX -- insufficient confirmed technical/price damage to flip to risk-off, but insufficient conviction to add risk either (crypto Extreme Fear despite green tape is a caution signal, not a buy signal, without a clearer capitulation or trend confirmation). REGIME UNCHANGED (neutral), heightened vigilance. Only action today: guardrail-driven SOL trim to hold the crypto sleeve at cap; otherwise a HOLD day pending clarity on the Strait of Hormuz situation and the Fed's actual rate path.

SPY.thesis: Core US equity beta at $751.83 (above $741.75 avgCost; +1.36%, ~13.06% NAV). Index near record (~7,540s) with VIX calm (~16.5) despite the Hormuz/oil shock and hawkish rate repricing -- market has not yet priced meaningful risk. Broad index remains the diversified anchor. HOLD, watch for technical deterioration if the geopolitical/oil situation worsens.
SPY.invalidationLevel: Sustained close below 660 USD or confirmed multi-hike path forcing a technical downtrend in the broad index.

NVDA.thesis: AI/compute secular leader at $211.80 (above $208.44 avgCost; +1.61%, ~14.60% NAV). Up ~4% July 14 leading the semis/AI complex (AVGO also +1.3%) even as broader macro risk (oil, rates) builds -- AI capex/data-center demand narrative intact and relative strength notable. Near the 15% cap -- no further adds. HOLD.
NVDA.invalidationLevel: AI capex digestion or data-center demand rollover; close below 150 USD.

MSFT.thesis: Quality AI/cloud compounder at $384.93 (below 391.98 avgCost; -1.80%, ~14.53% NAV). Near the 15% position cap -- no further adds. Azure + Copilot growth thesis unchanged. HOLD.
MSFT.invalidationLevel: Cloud growth deceleration or AI capex without demonstrable ROI; close below 340 USD.

GOOGL.thesis: Quality growth (search + cloud + AI) at $359.51 (below 366.53 avgCost; -1.91%, ~14.00% NAV). Search monopoly thesis intact; AI integration ongoing. HOLD, near cap.
GOOGL.invalidationLevel: Search share erosion to AI rivals causing revenue decline; close below 310 USD.

UNH.thesis: Defensive value at $425.19 (above $408.52 avgCost; +4.08%, ~6.19% NAV). Modest pullback from July 8 highs but still comfortably above avgCost; small position acting as a natural hedge against the current geopolitical/rate volatility. HOLD.
UNH.invalidationLevel: Further regulatory or medical-cost shock; close below 360 USD.

GLD.thesis: Tail/debasement hedge at $372.15 (below 386.54 avgCost; -3.72%, ~4.07% NAV). Rising 10Y real yields (4.62%, 2-month high) are a near-term headwind for gold even as the Hormuz/oil geopolitical shock argues for a hedge; net effect roughly a wash so far. Not adding here. HOLD.
GLD.invalidationLevel: Real yields spike with strong USD reversal sustained; close below 350 USD.

BTC.thesis: Core crypto sleeve leader at $64,777 (above 64,629 avgCost; +0.23%, ~14.40% NAV). Roughly flat vs. avgCost despite Fear & Greed falling to 25 'Extreme Fear' -- sentiment/price divergence (mcap +2.6% 24h while sentiment worsens) bears watching. BTC dominance rose to 56.3%. Crypto sleeve trimmed back to ~19.98% (neutral cap); no room to add. HOLD.

ETH.thesis: Discounted L1 leader at $1,883.72 (above 1,667.47 avgCost; +12.97%, ~4.48% NAV). Continuing to outperform BTC/SOL this week. Asymmetric upside intact when risk-on returns and crypto cap expands to 35%. HOLD.
ETH.invalidationLevel: Structural loss of share to L1 rivals; close below 1,300 USD.

SOL.thesis: High-beta satellite at $77.60 (above 67.81 avgCost; +14.44%, ~1.10% NAV after today's trim). Trimmed 2.6 SOL (~$202) to bring the crypto sleeve back under the 20% neutral cap after BTC/ETH-led drift; smallest-conviction sleeve name, first to trim on cap breaches. HOLD remainder for further upside on regime flip.
SOL.invalidationLevel: Break below 52 USD; network deterioration or sentiment collapse.
